nameMeanings.name: Ingleby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Ingleby (in Lincolnshire, Yorkshire, and Derbyshire). The placenames mean ‘farmstead of the English’, from Old English Engle ‘English’ (genitive plural Engla) + Old Norse bȳ ‘farmstead’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Ingleby was born in 1290. He married Edeline Ingleby about 1308. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1375, at the age of 85, and was buried in Ripley Churchyard, Ripley, Surrey, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Ingleby(은)는 1290년 태어났다. 그는 대략 1308년 Edeline Ingleby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1375년 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수리 리플리 리플리 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.
